reasoning.typography: Confident serif system with personality — the custom 'jsm' logotype and classical serif body text show deliberate typographic choices, not defaults
reasoning.hierarchy: Strong headline dominance and clear visual sections, though some mid-page density could use more breathing room between content blocks
reasoning.distinctiveness: Extremely specific — the type specimen showcases, book promotion, version timeline, and personal narrative couldn't belong to anyone else
reasoning.color: Masterful restraint — near-monochrome with strategic red accent on book cover and sparse decorative type specimens, classic design discipline
reasoning.density: Good rhythm but slightly inconsistent — hero section is spacious, mid-sections get denser with lists, version timeline breaks it up nicely but could use more confident emptiness
reasoning.voice: Structure directly reflects the person — type designer leads with type, book author promotes book, version history shows craft evolution, not generic 'services/testimonials' stencil

winning_patterns: Type specimen showcases (Citywide, Chronk) as actual portfolio pieces
Version history timeline showing iterative design thinking
Book promotion integrated as natural content, not CTA
Restrained near-monochrome palette with surgical accent use
anti_patterns: Mid-page list density (talks, interviews, articles) feels compressed
Section headers ('ABOUT ME', 'TYPE DESIGN') small-caps shouting lacks sophistication
Some generic left-sidebar content chunking in body sections
Past Versions section could be more visually dynamic given the concept
